popularStory.headline: BIO
popularStory.description:
BIO:  from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/ENGLISHNOBILITYMEDIEVAL3.htm#RichardFitzEustachedied1163B as of 7/15/2016

ROGER (-1211, bur Stanlow Priory).  A manuscript history of the Lacy family names …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Roger de Lacy (1170-1211), 6th Baron of Pontefract, 7th Lord of Bowland, Lord of Blackburnshire, 7th Baron of Halton and Constable of Chester (formerly Roger le Constable) was a notable English soldier, crusader and baron in the late 12th and early 13th centuries.

«b»Family and Provenance«/b»
Roger de Lacy was also known as Roger FitzJohn (son of John, constable of Chester) and during the time that he was hoping to inherit his grandmother's de Lisours lands as Roger de Lisours. He was the son of John FitzRichard (son of Richard), Baron of Halton, Lord of Bowland, Lord of Flamborough and Constable of Chester. Roger became Baron of Pontefract on the death of his paternal grandmother Albreda de Lisours (-aft.1194) who had inherited the Barony in her own right as 1st-cousin and heir to Robert de Lacy (-1193), 4th Baron of Pontefract. In agreements with his grandmother Roger adopted the name of de Lacy, received the right to inherit the Barony of Pontefract and its lands, and the lands of Bowland, and Blackburnshire. He gave up all claims to his grandmother's de Lisours lands. He also gave his younger brother Robert le Constable the Flamborough lands that he had inherited from his father. He married Maud (or Matilda) de Clere (not of the de Clare family).

«b»Service to Kings Henry, Richard and John«/b»
Robert de Lacy failed to support King Henry I during his power struggle with his brother and the King confiscated Pontefract Castle from the family during the 12th century. Roger paid King Richard I 3,000 marks for the Honour of Pontefract, but the King retained possession of the castle. He joined King Richard for the Third Crusade.

«b»Accession of King John«/b»
At the accession of King John of England, Roger was a person of great eminence, for we find him shortly after the coronation of that prince, deputed with the Sheriff of Northumberland, and other great men, to conduct William, King of Scotland, to Lincoln, where the English king had fixed to give him an interview. King John gave de Lacy Pontefract Castle in 1199, the year he ascended the throne.

«b»Military service«/b»
«b»Siege of Acre«/b»
Roger was the Constable of Chester, and joined Richard the Lionheart for the Third Crusade. Roger assisted at the Siege of Acre, in 1192 and clearly earned the favour and the trust of King Richard as a soldier and loyal subject as judged by his subsequent service.

«b»Château Gaillard«/b»
King Richard reconquered some castles along his Norman border from Philip II of France in 1196 and de Lacy was likely in his retinue. In 1203, de Lacy was the commander of the Château Gaillard in Normandy, when it was besieged and finally taken by Philip, marking the loss of mainland Normandy by the Plantagenêts. Under de Lacy's command the defence of the castle was lengthy, and it fell only after an eight-month siege on 8 March 1204. After the siege, de Lacy returned to England to begin work reinforcing Pontefract Castle.

«b»Siege of Rothelan«/b»
In the time of this Roger, Ranulph, Earl of Chester, having entered Wales at the head of some forces, was compelled, by superior numbers, to shut himself up in the castle of Rothelan (Rhuddlan Castle), where, being closely besieged by the Welsh, he sent for aid to the Constable of Chester. Hugh Lupus, the 1st Earl of Chester, in his charter of foundation of the Abbey of St. Werberg, at Chester, had given a privilege to the frequenters of Chester fair, "That they should not be apprehended for theft, or any other offense during the time of the fair, unless the crime was committed therein." This privilege made the fair, of course, the resort of thieves and vagabonds from all parts of the kingdom. Accordingly, the Constable, Roger de Lacy, forthwith marched to his relief, at the head of a concourse of people, then collected at the fair of Chester, consisting of minstrels, and loose characters of all description, forming altogether so numerous a body, that the besiegers, at their approach, mistaking them for soldiers, immediately raised the siege. For this timely service, the Earl of Chester conferred upon De Lacy and his heirs, the patronage of all the minstrels in those parts, which patronage the Constable transferred to his steward; and was enjoyed for many years afterwards.

«b»High Sheriff«/b»
He was appointed High Sheriff of Cumberland for the years 1204 to 1209.

«b»Death and succession«/b»
Roger died in 1211, and was succeeded by his son, John de Lacy, 2nd Earl of Lincoln.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger de Lacy於1170年1月出生在英國林可爾史爾林肯時，他的父親John de Lacy27歲，母親Alice of Essex34歲。他於1192年與Matilda de Clare結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1192年登記服兵役。他在英國藍卡爾被列為sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i。他去世於1212年1月享年42歲，埋葬在英國爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger de Lacy于1170年1月出生在英国林可爾史爾林肯时，他的父亲John de Lacy27岁，母亲Alice of Essex34岁。他于1192年与Matilda de Clare结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1192年登记服兵役。他在英国藍卡爾被列为sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i。他去世于1212年1月享年42岁，埋葬在英国爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lacy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lassy in Calvados, named from a Gaulish personal name Lascius (of uncertain origin and meaning) + the locative suffix -acum.

variants.name: Lasee
meanings: Probably an altered form of English or Irish   Lacy  .

variants.name: Lacey
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Lacy  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger de Lacy was born in January 1170, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, his father, John de Lacy, was 27 and his mother, Alice of Essex, was 34. He married Matilda de Clare in 1192. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1192. His occupation is listed as sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i in Lancashire, England. He died in January 1212, at the age of 42, and was buried in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger de Lacy est né en janvier 1170, à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Lacy, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alice of Essex, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Matilda de Clare en 1192. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1192. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i à Lancashire, Angleterre. Il est décédé en janvier 1212, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterré à Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger de Lacy im Januar 1170 in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Lacy 27 und seine Mutter Alice of Essex 34. Er hat 1192 Matilda de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1192 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. sein Beruf ist als sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i in Lancashire, England aufgeführt. Er starb im Januar 1212 im Alter von 42 Jahren und wurde in Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Lacy è nato il nel gennaio 1170 nel Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, John de Lacy, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Alice of Essex, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Matilda de Clare nel 1192. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1192. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i nel Lancashire, England. Morì nel gennaio 1212, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolto a Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger de Lacyが1170年1月にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンで生まれた時，父John de Lacyは27歳，母Alice of Essexは34歳であった。1192年にMatilda de Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1192年に兵役登録をした。イギリスランカシュアイアでの職業はsheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard iと記録されている。彼は1212年1月に42歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger de Lacy(이)가 1170년 1월 영국 링코실르 링코컨에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Lacy(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice of Essex(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1192년 Matilda de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1192년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그의 직업은 영국 랭카실르에서 sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i이었다. 그는 1212년 1월 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르에 묻혔다.

mn.January 1170–January 1212: 1170 оны Нэгдүгээр сар–1212 оны Нэгдүгээр сар

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Lacy nasceu em janeiro de 1170, em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Lacy, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Alice of Essex, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda de Clare em 1192. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1192. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i em Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em janeiro de 1212, com 42 anos, e foi sepultado em Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger de Lacy родился январь 1170 г. в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Lacy было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice of Essex, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda de Clare в 1192 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. В 1192 году он поступил на военную службу. Работал по профессии sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i в местности ланкашир, Англия. Он умер январь 1212 г. в возрасте 42 лет и был похоронен в местности чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger de Lacy nació en enero de 1170, en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Lacy, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Alice of Essex, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Matilda de Clare en 1192. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1192. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff of lancaster for 7th, 8th, and 9th of richard i en Lancashire, Inglaterra. Murió en enero de 1212, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultado en Cheshire, Inglaterra.
